ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for cheese foam bubble tea. FOODON_03301099 (tea beverage) is the closest defensible parent class. Cheese foam and tapioca pearl components are not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for cheese foam or black sugar bubble tea variants in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cheese foam bubble tea. SNOMED CT does not enumerate specialty beverage preparations at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one cup (500 g / 500 ml), which is the typical retail cup size for this beverage in Singapore bubble tea shops.

nutrient_source.title: Cheese Foam & Black Sugar Bubble Yingde Black Tea — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this specific beverage (cr_id G00010092). All per-100g values including energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, and cholesterol are taken verbatim from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from cream cheese and whipping cream composition using USDA FoodData Central reference data for cream cheese (FDC ID 173420) and heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859), as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for tapioca-based bubble tea
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cheese foam black sugar bubble tea. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant glycemic drivers: black sugar syrup (GI ~65, analogous to brown sugar, University of Sydney GI Database) and tapioca pearls (GI ~56–70, University of Sydney GI Database entry for tapioca). The cheese foam component (cream cheese, whipping cream) contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially affect GI. Composite GI weighted toward black sugar and tapioca components. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (65 × 44.25) / 100 = 28.8. Carbohydrate per serving = 8.85 g/100g × 500 g = 44.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Critically elevated sodium at 1279 mg/100g; a 500 g serving delivers approximately 6395 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg by more than three times.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension must avoid this beverage entirely; the sodium content per serving is clinically dangerous and far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 6.24 g sugar/100g exceeding the 5 g/100g SSB gate; tapioca pearls are high-GI refined starch; composite GI estimated at 65 with glycemic load of 28.8 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage; the combination of black sugar syrup, high-GI tapioca pearls, and liquid sugar delivery causes rapid postprandial glucose spikes that are incompatible with glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at approximately 13.5 g/100g from cream cheese and whipping cream; sodium at 1279 mg/100g; cholesterol at 78 mg/100g; all three are independent cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this beverage; the high saturated fat content raises LDL cholesterol, the extreme sodium load elevates blood pressure, and the combined effect significantly increases cardiovascular event risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat at approximately 13.5 g/100g promotes pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-6); black sugar and tapioca pearls are refined sugars that drive systemic inflammation; sodium at 1279 mg/100g exacerbates joint swelling.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage; the high saturated fat, refined sugar, and extreme sodium content collectively promote inflammatory pathways relevant to RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheese Foam and Black Sugar Bubble Yingde Black Tea is a specialty milk-tea beverage originating from Chinese bubble tea culture and widely sold in Singapore. It consists of three layered components: a base of Yingde black tea (a premium Chinese black tea from Guangdong province known for its malty, full-bodied character), a layer of black sugar syrup-coated tapioca pearls (boba), and a thick handcrafted cheese foam topping made from New Zealand cream cheese and dairy whipping cream. The cheese foam is savory, rich, and slightly salty, creating a distinctive contrast with the sweet black sugar pearls and the astringent tea base. The tapioca pearls are cooked using traditional methods to achieve a soft, chewy, and sticky texture. Nutritionally, this beverage is calorie-dense at 307 kcal per 100 g, with high protein (16.09 g/100g) and fat (23.06 g/100g) primarily from the cream cheese and whipping cream in the foam layer. Sodium is critically elevated at 1279 mg/100g, making a single 500 g serving deliver approximately 6395 mg sodium — more than three times the WHO daily recommended limit. Sugar is 6.24 g/100g, classifying this as a sugar-sweetened beverage. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 13.5 g/100g. Calcium is notably high at 1375 mg/100g from the dairy components. This beverage is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its extreme sodium, saturated fat, and sugar content. It is best consumed as an occasional indulgence by healthy adults.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with confidence 0.95 for this exact beverage entry (cr_id G00010092). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference data for cream cheese and whipping cream as HPB does not report this separately. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for black sugar and tapioca components.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (13.5 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference entries for cream cheese (FDC ID 173420, ~20.2 g sat fat/100g) and heavy whipping cream (FDC ID 170859, ~23.0 g sat fat/100g), weighted by approximate cheese foam composition. HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this specific cheese foam bubble tea preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a composite beverage entry as of 2026. 3. Dietary fibre is reported as 0.0 g/100g per HPB source; this is plausible given that tapioca pearls contain negligible fibre and the tea and dairy components contribute none. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific beverage exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. Halal status is marked false as the cheese foam uses dairy cream and cream cheese whose halal certification is not confirmed in the HPB record. 6. Calcium at 1375 mg/100g is exceptionally high and reflects the concentrated dairy content of the cheese foam layer; this value is taken verbatim from the HPB source.
